review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls relevant to the FedRAMP SC-7 (5) control. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but minor improvements in clarity and consistency could be considered. All functions are technically correct and align with the deny-by-default, allow-by-exception principle.
title: Boundary Protection | Deny by Default — Allow by Exception
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7 (5) focuses on boundary protection with a deny-by-default and allow-by-exception approach. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to network security configurations that restrict access by default and only allow specific, defined traffic. NetworkPolicies can enforce default deny rules for both ingress and egress traffic, ensuring that no traffic is allowed unless explicitly permitted. Additionally, restricting the use of LoadBalancer services and ensuring TLS is enabled for Ingress resources align with the principle of minimizing public exposure and securing access.
